name: Replication Through Removable Media
description: Adversaries may move onto systems by copying malware to removable media and taking advantage of Autorun features.

name: Exfiltration Over Physical Medium
description: Adversaries may attempt to exfiltrate data via a physical medium, such as a removable drive.

name: Hardware Additions
description: Adversaries may introduce malicious hardware (e.g., BadUSB) to gain access to networks.

name: Data Encrypted for Impact
description: Adversaries may encrypt data on target systems to interrupt availability (ransomware).

name: Inhibit System Recovery
description: Adversaries may delete or remove built-in OS recovery features (shadow copies, backups).

name: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
description: Adding entries to Run keys or Startup folder for persistence.

name: Scheduled Task/Job
description: Abuse task scheduling to execute malicious code at system startup or on a schedule.

name: PowerShell
description: Adversaries may abuse PowerShell commands and scripts for execution.

name: Visual Basic
description: Adversaries may abuse VBScript for execution.

name: Windows Command Shell
description: Abuse cmd.exe to execute commands and scripts.

name: Spearphishing Attachment
description: Malicious attachments sent via email to gain initial access.

name: Malicious File
description: User execution of a malicious file (double-click, open).

name: Obfuscated Files or Information
description: Encrypt, encode, or obfuscate payloads to evade detection.

name: Deobfuscate/Decode Files
description: Use utilities to deobfuscate or decode files or information.

name: Masquerading
description: Manipulate features to make malware appear legitimate (filename, icon).

name: Process Injection
description: Inject code into a running process to evade defenses and elevate privileges.

name: OS Credential Dumping
description: Attempt to dump credentials from the OS (LSASS, SAM, etc.).

name: Unsecured Credentials
description: Search compromised systems for insecurely stored credentials.

name: Remote Services
description: Use valid accounts to log into remote services (RDP, SSH, SMB).

name: Lateral Tool Transfer
description: Transfer tools between systems within a compromised environment.

name: Application Layer Protocol
description: Communicate using application layer protocols (HTTP, HTTPS, DNS).

name: Ingress Tool Transfer
description: Transfer tools from an external system into the compromised environment.

name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
description: Steal data by exfiltrating it over the existing C2 channel.

name: Valid Accounts
description: Use stolen or valid credentials to maintain access.

name: Impair Defenses
description: Disable or tamper with security tools (antivirus, firewall, logging).

name: Modify Registry
description: Interact with the Windows Registry to hide configuration or aid persistence.

name: Indicator Removal
description: Delete or modify artifacts to remove evidence of intrusion.

name: Virtualization/Sandbox Evasion
description: Detect and avoid virtualization and sandbox environments.

name: System Services
description: Abuse system services for execution (service creation/modification).

name: System Binary Proxy Execution
description: Use signed binaries to proxy execution (rundll32, mshta, etc.).

name: Create or Modify System Process
description: Create or modify system-level processes for persistence.

name: Account Manipulation
description: Modify accounts to maintain access (add credentials, change permissions).

name: Archive Collected Data
description: Compress and/or encrypt collected data prior to exfiltration.

name: Automated Collection
description: Use automated techniques to collect internal data.

name: Screen Capture
description: Take screen captures of the victim's desktop.

name: Input Capture
description: Capture user input (keylogging, credential interception).

name: System Information Discovery
description: Attempt to get detailed information about the OS and hardware.

name: File and Directory Discovery
description: Enumerate files and directories on the target system.

name: Process Discovery
description: Get information about running processes on a system.

name: System Network Configuration Discovery
description: Look for details about network configuration (interfaces, IPs, routes).

name: System Network Connections Discovery
description: Attempt to get a listing of network connections (netstat).

name: Software Discovery
description: Attempt to get a listing of software and versions installed.

name: Query Registry
description: Interact with the Windows Registry to gather information.

name: System Service Discovery
description: Get information about registered services.

name: System Time Discovery
description: Gather system time and time zone information.

name: System Location Discovery
description: Gather information about the victim system's geographic location.

name: Account Access Removal
description: Interrupt availability by removing access to accounts.

name: Service Stop
description: Stop or disable services to render them unavailable.

name: System Shutdown/Reboot
description: Shutdown or reboot the system to interrupt access or aid destruction.

name: Data Destruction
description: Destroy data and files on specific systems or across a network.
